calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment; Discharge Forfeiture And Exonerate Bail, Etc.
Off Calendar Jan-15-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FINANCIAL CASUALTY & SURETY, INC.'s Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment; Discharge Forfeiture And Exonerate Bail, Etc.

Defendant Financial Casualty & Surety, Inc.'s Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment; Discharge Forfeiture And Exonerate Bai is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Where, as here, bail is ordered forfeited, the clerk must timely mail notice of the forfeiture to the surety and the bail agent, as well as execute a certificate of mailing documenting their actions. (Pen. Code section 1305(b).) Defendant has submitted credible evidence that it never received notice of forfeiture. (See Ramos Decl.) The City and County of San Francisco filed an untimely opposition, but even there, the City did not produce evidence that a timely mailing occurred or a certificate of mailing was executed. It follows Defendant was without notice of the summary judgment proceedings that led to the judgment. The judgment, thus, is vacated. (See CCP section 473.) The time for Defendant to respond to the forfeiture by, for example, moving to set aside forfeiture of the bond and to exonerate bail, is extended. The statutory time shall commence from date the court files this order. The motion is denied to the extent Defendant seeks different or additional relief.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301/JMQ)
